title: Adding Enclosures in Endorsements
source_text: 4. Adding Enclosures. Do not repeat an enclosure in the enclosure line of your endorsement that has already been identified in the enclosure line of the basic letter or a previous endorsement. Identify only the enclosures that you add. Assign a number to all enclosures you add by continuing the sequence of numbers from the basic letter and previous endorsements. For example, if the basic letter and previous endorsements had enclosures identified up to number "(3)," the first enclosure of your endorsement would be number "(4)."
plain_language_rule: Do not repeat enclosures that have already been identified in the basic letter or previous endorsements. Identify only the enclosures you add. Assign numbers to new enclosures by continuing the sequence from the basic letter and previous endorsements (e.g., if prior enclosures end at "(3)," your first new enclosure is "(4)").
renderer_impact: Endorsement enclosure line must not repeat enclosures from the basic letter or prior endorsements. New enclosures continue the number sequence from the basic letter and previous endorsements.
notes: Source text resolved from SECNAV M-5216.5 CH-1, 9-2.4 Adding Enclosures. Key points: (1) no repetition of prior enclosures, (2) identify only new enclosures, (3) continue number sequence from basic letter and previous endorsements.
